reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified standalone AND on-page in headless Chrome; real classification head [1,201] with correct top language across 6 languages; auto-init; desktop+mobile layout checked at 0px overflow), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED twice (headless Chrome, WASM, transformers.js@3.7.5 CDN build). Standalone probe: pipeline('text-classification','onnx-community/language_detection-ONNX',{dtype:'q8'}) returned the correct top language on 7/7 multi-language samples — fra_Latn 0.998, deu_Latn 0.9999, eng_Latn 0.983, spa_Latn 0.995, jpn_Jpan 0.9999, rus_Cyrl 0.916, ita_Latn 0.981 — and AutoModelForSequenceClassification returned real logits with dims [1, 201] (sample values 0.462, 1.318, 0.308, -4.059). On the built page: model auto-loaded and detected across en/fr/ja/ar/ko/zu (eng_Latn 99.2%, fra_Latn 99.7%, jpn_Jpan 100%, ars_Arab 67.1%, kor_Hang 100%, zul_Latn 99.3%), latency ~6 ms, ZERO console errors.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker; full softmax over all 201 classes returned via top_k: 201.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = a real classification head: logits shape [1, 201] → softmax → sorted [{label, score}] over 201 FLORES-200 codes. Verified directly (out.logits.dims === [1,201]) and via the pipeline's sorted score list. NOT a head-less encoder (last_hidden_state) — a genuine 201-way language classifier.
rubric.notes: Labels are FLORES-200 codes (iso639-3_Script). The page shows friendly names for common ones and an honest parsed 'iso · Script' fallback for the long tail (e.g. ars_Arab rendered 'ars · Arabic'); the raw code is always shown.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (onnx-community/language_detection-ONNX, linked), the base (alexneakameni/language_detection), the task (text-classification), architecture (BertForSequenceClassification, multilingual BERT), backend (WASM), quantisation (q8, model_quantized.onnx — verified non-degenerate), size (~28 MB) and license (MIT, base model). Inference is off the main thread.
rubric.notes: dtype q8 disclosed AND validated: 7/7 correct with high confidence — no degenerate collapse. Materially distinct from the built XLM-RoBERTa 20-language demo (different architecture + 201 vs 20 labels).

rubric.evidence: The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (modelId onnx-community/language_detection-ONNX, sizeMB 28). Verified on-page: the loader auto-initialised and detection ran on load; a Clear-cached-model control is exposed once ready. Absent model would show Download (no silent auto-download of a large model).
rubric.notes: At ~28 MB the q8 build is small enough to be practical on mobile, unlike the 270 MB XLM-R detector.

rubric.evidence: Loading/detecting state is announced in role=status aria-live regions; the shared loader exposes a Retry recovery path on failure; a Detecting… status line reports progress and errors surface on the page ('Detection failed: …'). Console + network stayed clean across load + detection.
rubric.notes: Debounced detect-as-you-type (350 ms) keeps the input responsive.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: the text textarea (detect-on-input), language sample chips (English/Français/日本語/العربية/한국어/isiZulu/Code-switch), and per-page controls — practical adds a multi-line messages box + Detect & route button; wild adds tricky-example chips; multi-model adds a Detect & translate button chaining a MarianMT translator.
rubric.notes: Sample chips exercise the wide net — Korean and Zulu are NOT in the built 20-language XLM-R demo, making the distinctness tangible.

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the worker runs the real transformers.js text-classification pipeline; the top result, probability bars and the FLORES codes are all derived from the real 201-way softmax. The standalone probe confirmed the exact logits [1,201] the UI renders.
rubric.notes: Long-tail languages with no curated name show the honest parsed code (e.g. ars · Arabic), never a fabricated name.

rubric.evidence: All chips/buttons have accessible names; results use aria-live regions; the probability bars are labelled with language name + FLORES code + percentage. Light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system.

Verified 0px horizontal overflow at mobile (360×740, DPR3, touch) and clean desktop (1280×800) via scripts/responsive-check. The bar grid uses minmax(8rem,12rem) 1fr auto and ellipsis on long language labels; chips flex-wrap; textareas are inline-size:100%.
rubric.notes: support seeded via responsive-check; sub-24px flags are inline prose/table links (WCAG target-size exempt).

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a Web Worker; a single BERT forward pass over a short input classifies in ~6 ms on WASM (measured on-page). The bar render is a bounded top-6 slice of the sorted distribution — no main-thread inference, no unbounded loop.
rubric.notes: INP kept low — detection never blocks the interface even when typing.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low; run text classification off the main thread without janking input
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move inference off the main thread; keep any main-thread loop bounded.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — classification runs in a dedicated module Web Worker; the bar render is a bounded top-6 slice; detect-as-you-type is debounced (350 ms).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread; ~6 ms latency measured on-page; console clean; UI responsive while typing

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive result list / probability bars without horizontal overflow on narrow mobile
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid grid with intrinsic min sizing (minmax) + text-overflow ellipsis; logical properties; flex-wrap chips; avoid fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — li-bar-row grid minmax(8rem,12rem) 1fr auto; long language labels ellipsis; chips flex-wrap; textarea inline-size:100%.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0px mobile overflow measured at 360×740 in headless Chrome via responsive-check

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates for detection results and labelled probability bars
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; label every control; give each bar an accessible name.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + page status use role=status aria-live; chips/buttons carry accessible names; bars label name + FLORES code + percentage.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.

openQuestions: The curated friendly-name map covers ~100 of the 201 FLORES codes; the long tail shows the honest parsed 'iso · Script' fallback. Worth expanding the map for more of the low-resource languages that are the whole point of the 201-language net.

On closely related same-script pairs (e.g. Croatian/Bosnian/Serbian-Latin, or Arabic regional variants) the model spreads probability — worth a dedicated 'confusable languages' surface quantifying that spread.

followUpGoals.goal: Expand the FLORES-200 friendly-name map to cover more of the low-resource long tail so the 201-language breadth reads as names, not codes, for more inputs.
Add a 'confusable languages' wild surface that quantifies how the softmax mass splits across closely related same-script languages (e.g. hr/bs/sr-Latn, Arabic variants).

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for mbert-language-detection (onnx-community/language_detection-ONNX, text-classification, q8, ~28 MB — a multilingual BERT / BertForSequenceClassification fine-tune, base alexneakameni/language_detection, over 201 FLORES-200 languages). A materially DISTINCT model from the built 20-language XLM-RoBERTa 'language-identification' demo: different architecture and ~10x the languages. REAL classification head VERIFIED — logits shape [1,201] (standalone), correct top language 7/7 (fr/de/en/es/ja/ru/it) standalone and 6/6 on-page (en/fr/ja/ar/ko/zu), ~6 ms latency, 0px mobile overflow, ZERO console/network errors. Ladder: basics (detect a language + full distribution), practical (route messages to per-language queues), wild (short/ambiguous/code-switched), multi-model (detect then route to the matching MarianMT translator into English, keyed on FLORES codes). License MIT (base model).
